mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.24% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevation of FEDFUNDS above 4.0% signals continued credit tightening that depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 5.0% combined with month-over-month payroll growth deceleration signals labor market softening. When PAYEMS growth falls below 50,000 persons per month while UNRATE remains elevated, equity investors anticipate recession, triggering negative forward returns within 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 50,000 persons AND UNRATE >= 4.8%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138,495k and UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2007-12
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 382,928 million dollars reflects consumer spending momentum. A sustained 3-month decline in RSAFS growth rate below 2% year-over-year signals demand destruction and inventory correction, producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings guidance is revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 60-day returns is < 0.10 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) at 113.9 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. A decline in INDPRO below its 6-month moving average combined with rising CPIAUCSL signals stagflation conditions where supply constraints meet weakening demand, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days as margin compression is anticipated.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.9 and CPIAUCSL at 210.565 as of 2007-11
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.15), or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of stagflation-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,187 thousand units represents residential construction activity. A sharp month-over-month decline in HOUST below 1,100 thousand signals housing market collapse and negative wealth effects on consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as financial sector stress emerges.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market deterioration visible in late 2007
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in > 50% of instances when HOUST < 1,100k, or Rank IC between HOUST and forward returns is < 0.05 in absolute value over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 138,495 thousand persons is the primary labor market indicator. Sustained month-over-month payroll declines signal recession onset. When PAYEMS declines for two consecutive months, equity investors reprrice earnings expectations downward, producing negative forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following consecutive PAYEMS declines, or the lagged PAYEMS decline signal has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 210.565 combined with elevated FEDFUNDS at 4.24% creates a real interest rate environment that constrains equity valuations. When the spread between FEDFUNDS and CPI growth widens above 1.5%, the Fed's restrictive stance becomes binding, producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.24% and CPIAUCSL at 210.565 as of 2007-12
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with real rate tightening
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPI growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.12), or SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 60% of high real-rate instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11,658.9 billion chained 2017 dollars represents the broadest demand measure. Quarterly GDP growth deceleration below 1.5% annualized combined with declining RSAFS signals demand exhaustion. This regime produces negative equity returns over 60 trading days as recession probability rises and terminal growth expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% annualized AND RSAFS 3-month growth < 2.5%
failure_condition: The joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15), or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when both conditions are triggered.
